rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KUEI-SHI LEI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/16/2023. (1) Need clarification the relief Petition seeks. Petition #1.a sets forth that petitioner requests determination of property passing to the surviving spouse without administration; however, Petition #7.b is affirmed, not #7.a. (2) Need allegation in Attachment 7 setting forth all facts upon which Petitioner bases. This is a newly filed petition. All necessary information must be included even though relevant information was provided in another case. (3) Proposed Order must be submitted prior to the hearing. (4) Petition and Proposed Order need to clearly identify to whom the property will pass in what capacity as the surviving spouse passed away. The procedural issues must be cleared two weeks prior to the hearing. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/28/2023, as the following issues remain unaddressed (see tentative ruling for 06/12/2023 hearing): 1) All the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) must be provided. 2) No "final" inventory and appraisal appears to have been filed. 3) Please state the amount of the estate cash on hand and provide a proof that the cash including the net proceeds from the sale of the estate real property is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise held in the form of investment(s) authorized by Probate Code section 9730. An appearance may be required on 08/28/2023 if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD E. ANDERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Still no Letters or Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits have been filed for the post-deceased heir. (2) Statutory fee base still includes receipts that were partially reclaimed, and the administrator hired and paid his daughter. (3) Administrator's expenses are still not fully described. (4) The reserve requested is $5,000, but the prayer shows $20,000. (5) The attorney's fees do not appear to be mathematically correct, and extraordinary services in connection with the petition filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 11700 do not appear to have been reasonable or in the best interests of the estate. (6) Administrator apparently abandoned or discarded tangible personal property without giving Notice of Proposed Action. (7) The proposed distribution amounts appear to be incorrect, and there is no schedule showing the calculation. [Probate Code Section 1063(f) and CRC 7.651(a).] Also, proposed distribution of cash cannot be ordered when the estate still holds T-bills. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHELE PAYNE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By Administrator To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Royalty Interests And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate And Heirs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed since this petition was initially set for 02/01/2023: 1) No proposed order (which must be a one-sided hard copy) has been received (no courtesy copy of the 71-page petition (which must also be a one-sided hard copy) has been received, either). 2) Crosspointe 2, LLC (and Teichert if applicable) must be served at least 30 days before the hearing in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) & CCP sections 413.10 et seq. 3) Item 3 of the DE-115 form must provide a sufficient description of the subject property ("LLC" is not the subject property - royalty interest, right to receive royalties, and/or other contractual rights arising under Crosspointe's agreement with Crosspointe 2 are). 4) No proof has been filed to confirm that AJ Salomon's 50% interest in the LLC passed to the decedent (spousal property order, order for final distribution, assignment of interest, etc.). If there is no such proof, AJ Salomon's heirs (as he reportedly died intestate without probate administration pending or anticipated) must be served with notice and this petition in the same manner as Crosspointe 2, LLC (Teichert) under section 851(a)(2). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHELSEA JULIA ANNE CURRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, because the administrator is the sole heir, although she alleges that she did not sell, purchase, or exchange any assets, but the assets held in the Robinhood account are reportedly no longer on hand, and her attorney, not she, the fiduciary, signed and verified the declaration filed on 6/14/23. [CRC 7.103(c).] (Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own and not in a "Declaration in Response to Examiner's Notes." The current market value of securities do not need to be set forth, only the shares of such assets is required, as the value of securities fluctuates.)

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues not cleared. 1. Notice of Hearing (JC Form DE-120) and a copy of Petition to be given to all heirs at law in the manner provided in CCP 413.10 et seq pursuant to Prob. Code 17203(b). , all heirs at law must be noticed in the manner provided in CCP 413.10 et seq. as Petitioner requests the trust assets to be distributed pursuant to intestate law. 2. Petition must suggest a proposed fiduciary with his/her written consent to act. The court will not name one. 3. Proposed Order to be submitted. rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Proof of service of notice of directions for remote appearances not on file. LRSF 14.1.D.6). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TSIU KING LEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/27/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Who is the successor-in-interest to the post-deceased son? (LRSF 14.18 D.3.] (2) How are the petitioner and the other cousin of the post-deceased son the decedent's cousins? Who were the grandparents and the parents through whom the cousins might take? [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] (3) What are the relationships to the decedent of Ying Yi Yuen and Michael Ming Yi Yuen? (4) No declaration has been filed yet, setting forth the heirs, as alleged on Attachment 3g(2)(a). Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Items 6 and 7 of the petition do not need to be completed when issue survived.)
